title: trisetum spicatum subsp. phleoides
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. phleoides, also known as Australian hair grass, is a type of grass native to Australia and parts of Asia, characterized by its distinctive hair-like awns and feathery plumes. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Trisetum spicatum subsp. phleoides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
